description: Labeled routing prompts: expected = skill that should handle the prompt ('none' = no skill should fire). kind: direct = obvious phrasing, paraphrase = no signature keywords, ambiguous = plausibly matches a sibling skill (hardest). Improvised corpus — no real usage history existed when authored (2026-07-05).

cases.prompt: answer in as few tokens as possible from now on
stop being wordy, compress every reply
keep your responses short
review this PR before I merge it
look over my diff and flag anything risky or out of scope
can you check my changes
create a new skill that formats SQL files
I want to add a custom slash command that scaffolds React components
extend the agent with a reusable capability
log what we did today in the daily log
note today's progress in the project journal
let's wrap up — record where we got to
this test is failing, figure out why and fix it
the app crashes on startup since yesterday
something is off with the output, it used to work
set the north star goal for this repo
what are we trying to achieve in this project
define our long-term objective
grill me with clarifying questions before you start
interview me about this idea until the ambiguity is gone
make sure you actually understand what I want first
create a jira ticket for this work
prepare a payload for the sprint board with a safe dry run
we need to track this for handoff
take this feature from research to verified implementation autonomously
handle the whole thing end to end: research, plan, build, review, verify
just build the whole feature for me
stop over-engineering, give me the simplest thing that works
yagni — do less, no extra abstractions
this solution feels bloated
build a disposable prototype to test whether this data model holds up
spike something throwaway so we can answer the layout question
try something quick before we commit to the design
open the site in a real browser and check the signup flow works
click through checkout and confirm each page loads
QA the web app for me
keep working on this one task in a loop until it is actually done
iterate on the fix until the tests pass, do not stop at the first attempt
just get it done, whatever it takes
write an implementation-ready plan with risks and acceptance criteria
break this down into ordered slices before we touch code
how should we approach this change
research how auth works in this codebase and write up a document
do a deep dive into the payment module and document the architecture
figure out how this subsystem actually works
check the PR status every 15 minutes on a schedule
re-run this prompt every morning even after a reboot
keep an eye on the deploy for me
capture the correction patterns from this session and draft a skill from them
turn the feedback I kept giving you into a reusable project skill
remember how I like things done for next time
/slack deploy finished, all green
tell slack the deploy finished
add this feature test-first with red-green-refactor
write the failing tests first, then make them pass
make sure the new behavior is properly tested
teach me rust over the next few weeks in short lessons
I want ongoing multi-session lessons on distributed systems
help me learn this topic over time
split the approved plan across parallel tmux panes so I can watch the agents
run these workstreams concurrently where I can see each worker
divide and conquer this plan
run an adversarial QA pass for regressions and edge cases
try to break what I just built
is this actually solid?
fix all 200 lint errors across the repo as a batch
apply the same small change to many files
there are lots of little independent tasks piled up
prove the thing you just claimed is done, with fresh evidence
show me it actually works before you say done
done? really?
run the live BYOK end-to-end check before merging this hooks PR
drive the omp change on a real model and capture evidence
do a full end-to-end check of the change
fan this question out to several models and synthesize a weighted verdict
get a multi-model second opinion with a minority report
which of these two options should we pick
set up a git worktree so I can work on both branches at once
start the new ticket without switching my current branch
check out that PR locally so I can poke at it
what time is it in Tokyo
